name: Return to the Santa Teresa Reservoir — circular from Guijuelo
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse de Santa Teresa — circular desde Guijuelo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Guijuelo offers routes through varied terrain, from disused railway lines converted into Vías Verdes to the open forests of "dehesas." The region features quiet roads alongside rivers and reservoir lakes, providing scenic views. While Vías Verdes offer relatively flat sections, the broader area includes hills and middle mountains, particularly towards the Sierra de Francia and the foothills of the Sierra de Béjar. This diverse landscape provides options for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Guijuelo The most popular touring cycling route is Fuenterroble de Salvatierra and Los Santos Loop, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Return to the Santa Teresa Reservoir — circular from Guijuelo, a difficult 38.9 miles (62.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Santa Teresa Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Béjar from Guijuelo — circular via EuroVelo 1, a 40.0 miles (64.4 km) trail leading through the regional landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Guijuelo is defined by Vías Verdes, "dehesas" landscapes, and hilly terrain. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guijuelo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Guijuelo?
A: There are 18 touring cycling routes available around Guijuelo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. Most of these routes are considered difficult, with 16 routes falling into this category, while 2 are moderate.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Guijuelo?
A: Guijuelo offers a diverse cycling terrain. You'll find sections of "Vías Verdes" (disused railway lines converted into cycle paths) like the Ruta de la Plata Greenway, which provide relatively flat and traffic-free riding. The area also features picturesque "dehesas" landscapes with holm oaks, quiet roads alongside rivers and reservoir lakes, and more challenging hilly and middle-mountain terrain, especially towards the Sierra de Francia and the foothills of the Sierra de Béjar.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Guijuelo?
A: While many routes in the area are rated as difficult, the presence of "Vías Verdes" offers sections that are generally flatter and traffic-free, making them more accessible for beginners or families. These converted railway lines provide a gentler introduction to touring cycling in the region.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging options. The region includes hills and middle mountains, particularly towards the Sierra de Francia and the Sierra de Béjar foothills. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043074058/bejar-from-guijuelo-circular-via-eurovelo-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar from Guijuelo — circular via EuroVelo 1&lt;/a&gt; route is rated as difficult, covering 40 miles (64.4 km) with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes near Guijuelo?
A: Cycling routes often pass through scenic "dehesas" landscapes, alongside rivers like the Tormes, and offer views of reservoir lakes, such as those seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042325443/return-to-the-santa-teresa-reservoir-circular-from-guijuelo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Santa Teresa Reservoir — circular from Guijuelo&lt;/a&gt; route. The broader region also features the ancient oak and chestnut forests of the Sierra de Francia.

Q: Are there any cultural landmarks or points of interest to explore in Guijuelo?
A: Yes, after your ride, you can explore Guijuelo's cultural side. The town is famous for its Iberian ham industry, and you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4236728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guijuelo Jamón District&lt;/a&gt; or the Museum of the Iberian Ham Industry. Other points of interest include the Plaza Mayor and Plaza de Julián Coca. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1690677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granite Theme Park&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Guijuelo?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047771823/fuenterroble-de-salvatierra-and-los-santos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenterroble de Salvatierra and Los Santos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042325443/return-to-the-santa-teresa-reservoir-circular-from-guijuelo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Santa Teresa Reservoir — circular from Guijuelo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043074058/bejar-from-guijuelo-circular-via-eurovelo-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar from Guijuelo — circular via EuroVelo 1&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Guijuelo?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for touring cycling, offering milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters might bring colder conditions, especially in the higher elevations towards the Sierras.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists near the touring routes in Guijuelo?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Guijuelo is a town with local amenities. You can typically find parking within the town itself, which serves as a common starting point for many routes, including those that are circular from Guijuelo.

Q: What are some of the most popular touring cycling routes in the Guijuelo area?
A: Among the most popular routes are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047771823/fuenterroble-de-salvatierra-and-los-santos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenterroble de Salvatierra and Los Santos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 25.7-mile (41.3 km) ride, and the more difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042325443/return-to-the-santa-teresa-reservoir-circular-from-guijuelo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Santa Teresa Reservoir — circular from Guijuelo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling paths or networks connected to Guijuelo?
A: Yes, Guijuelo is integrated into a network of "Vías Verdes," which are disused railway lines converted into cycle paths. A notable example is the Ruta de la Plata Greenway. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043074058/bejar-from-guijuelo-circular-via-eurovelo-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar from Guijuelo — circular via EuroVelo 1&lt;/a&gt; route indicates a connection to the EuroVelo 1 long-distance cycling network, offering possibilities for extended tours.
tips: Nice stretch between these towns on the banks of the reservoir. brand new asphalt
Quiet road with beautiful views of the mountains

This is the tourist and gastronomic area of Guijuelo, considered the birthplace of acorn-fed Iberian ham with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO Guijuelo).

More than 150 ham and cured meat producers are concentrated here. The cold, dry climate of the Sierra de Béjar favors a slow, natural curing process, giving the ham a sweeter, more unctuous flavor.
